Are you sure you want to remove this pro: Are you sure you want to remove this project?
Hourly chart: lines and opens divide eac: Hourly chart: lines and opens divide each local day by 24 (estimated).
Click Save to apply dashboard settings.
Connecting to remote projects requires t: Connecting to remote projects requires the Remote Development extension pack by Microsoft. Automatic authentication currently depends on setting up your .ssh/config file.
Failed to clone Git repository. Check th: Failed to clone Git repository. Check the URL and your permissions.
Failed to create GitHub repository. Make: Failed to create GitHub repository. Make sure the GitHub CLI (gh) is installed and authenticated.
Easy Dashboard: the web UI bundle is mis: Easy Dashboard: the web UI bundle is missing (local/dist). Run npm run build:webview then reload the window.
Enter the fully qualified URI string tha: Enter the fully qualified URI string that connects to the project. You can use this to connect to anything your editor can connect to.
Leave empty to disable — browse or type: Leave empty to disable — browse or type an absolute path
Local projects and folders stay in sync: Local projects and folders stay in sync with this directory. When you set or change it, existing local projects are moved to match the dashboard tree.
No activity data yet. Start working on p: No activity data yet. Start working on projects to see stats!
Create a project or folder to get starte: Create a project or folder to get started.
Open a project from the Projects view: Open a project from the Projects view
Open projects in new windows by default.
Open the dashboard when opening a new wi: Open the dashboard when opening a new window with a blank workspace.
Pick an icon or PNG file (optional): Pick an icon or PNG file (optional)
Projects and folders matched the root fo: Projects and folders matched the root folder.
Same as project name if empty...
Set a projects root folder in Easy Dashb: Set a projects root folder in Easy Dashboard settings first.
Set all project colours to a sequence st: Set all project colours to a sequence starting with the folder colour.
Set all projects in this folder to the f: Set all projects in this folder to the folder colour.
Set all projects to random colours based: Set all projects to random colours based on the folder colour.
Try changing or clearing the search: Try changing or clearing the search
Use the toolbar or context menu to add a: Use the toolbar or context menu to add a folder or project
Use the preset column buttons or craft y: Use the preset column buttons or craft your own using Bootstrap classes.
Leave empty to create under dashboard ro: Leave empty to create under dashboard root...
With a root folder set, leave “Project p: With a root folder set, leave “Project path” empty to create this project under the dashboard root. Pick a folder to add an existing project instead.
Your local coding activity — last 14 day: Your local coding activity — last 14 days (local date).
Your local coding activity — last 48 hou: Your local coding activity — last 48 hours (UTC).
Link or create a project on Vercel: Link or create a project on Vercel
The Vercel CLI links this folder to the: The Vercel CLI links this folder to the project name below (it creates the project on Vercel if it does not exist yet). By default the name matches the project name.
Requires the Vercel CLI and a token in E: Requires the Vercel CLI and a token in Easy Dashboard settings (recommended).
Create or name a new Vercel project (CLI: Create or name a new Vercel project (CLI creates it if missing)
Link an existing Vercel project (list fr: Link an existing Vercel project (list from your account)
Uses the Vercel REST API with the same t: Uses the Vercel REST API with the same team scope as in settings (personal account if team is empty).
Set Vercel API token in Easy Dashboard s: Set Vercel API token in Easy Dashboard settings to list projects.
Domains are loaded from your Cloudflare: Domains are loaded from your Cloudflare account. DNS is created as CNAME to Vercel without Cloudflare proxy (required for Vercel).
Add a Cloudflare API token in Easy Dashb: Add a Cloudflare API token in Easy Dashboard settings to list zones.
Tokens are stored in VS Code Secret Stor: Tokens are stored in VS Code Secret Storage. Leave token fields empty to keep the current values.
The slug and the team ID are two differe: The slug and the team ID are two different strings for the same team. The Vercel CLI accepts either one — paste only one value here (not both). Leave empty for a personal account.
Leave empty to keep the current token.
Could not list Cloudflare zones. Check t: Could not list Cloudflare zones. Check the token and network connection.
Could not list Vercel projects. Check th: Could not list Vercel projects. Check the token, team scope, and network connection.
Could not list Neon projects. Check the: Could not list Neon projects. Check the API key, organization ID, and network connection.
Neon operation did not finish in time.
Neon project was created but the respons: Neon project was created but the response did not include a project id.
Neon did not return a connection string: Neon did not return a connection string for this project.
Set Cloudflare API token in Easy Dashboa: Set Cloudflare API token in Easy Dashboard settings.
